Machine Overview
The ATF ���� is a large format, high-capacity automatic inline thermoforming machine
purpose-built for the high-speed production of thermoformed �D automotive car
mats and floor liners. With a generous ���� x ���� mm forming area, the machine is
ideally dimensioned to produce full vehicle mat sets — front driver, front passenger,
and rear mats — in a continuous automated cycle, delivering the deep draw capability
required for high-rim car mats with anti-slip surfaces and integrated water/dirt
retention recesses.
Thermoformable material is reel-fed into a �-pitch top-heating pre-heat station and
then vacuum formed. The single top oven configuration with two sequential heating
stations provides progressive, controlled heating optimised for the heavy-gauge
materials (TPE, TPO, PE, EVA) typically used in automotive floor liner production.
Multi-cavity formings are separated from the plastic web by a double acting guillotine,
and the thermoformed sheets are pushed out via a delivery conveyor — all in a single
continuous automated process.
The ���� x ���� mm forming area comfortably accommodates the largest car mat
dimensions encountered in the automotive aftermarket, including SUV and estate
vehicle sets where rear mat widths can exceed ���� mm and individual mat lengths
reach ���� mm.

b. Easy Tool Setup: The vacuum forming tool is usually present on the lower table of the

Tool Adjustment & Clamping
The tool is expected to be on top of a base that can slide on the Ball Transfer Units
(BTUs) on the bottom platen. The Ball Transfer Units raise up due to the air cylinders
present below them, during tool change using a hand valve. The Bottom Table can then
go up and the Universal Frames can be used to center the tool. Once the tool is centered
the bottom table can go down, the BTUs can go down and the tool base has to be bolted
on the bottom platen.
c. Front Door Locking: The Front Door is locked using air cylinders so it is easy to open &
close the Chamber below the sheet line.

c. Zero-Sag Control: Once the sheet is heated, the level of the sheet is controlled using
pulsated air blown in the chamber below the thermoplastic sheet.
The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming
area is completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– an
important feature to achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds.
“Zero Sag '' feature using pulsated air is present in the machine – using pulsated air – in the
chamber – with a pneumatic cylinder system – controllable on the HMI by PLC. The
machine also has a light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of the
heated sheet – to protect the sheet from touching the bottom heater and also to prevent
the sheet from sagging. Preblow photocell is above the sheet. Sag photocell is below the
sheet

Machine Quotation

Prepared By: Machinecraft Technologies Pvt. Ltd.
Key Account Manager: Rushabh Doshi - rushabh@machinecraft.org
1. Machine Overview
Machinecraft’s PF1 Series is a heavy-gauge, single-station cut-sheet thermoforming machine
line designed for versatility and high performance. Each PF1 machine is custom-built to client
requirements, with sizes ranging from compact ~800 × 1000 mm formats up to extra-large
5000 × 2500 mm formats. All models feature a robust closed-chamber design that prevents
sheet sag by allowing pre-blow air pressure control, ensuring superior forming quality on thick
materials. The PF1 Series delivers precision forming for a variety of thermoplastic sheets
(typically 2–12 mm thick), making it ideal for automotive, aerospace, industrial, and commercial
applications that demand excellent detail and consistency.
1

[PAGE 2]
● Rugged Construction & Intelligent Control: Built with a heavy-duty steel frame and
precision motion components, the PF1 ensures stability and durability. A modern PLC
control system with an intuitive HMI touchscreen comes standard, allowing easy
control of heating zones, vacuum timing, and motion sequences.
● Classic Configuration: In its classic configuration, the PF1 includes sandwich heating
ovens in top & bottom from the sheet line - (ceramic infrared elements) with solid-state
temperature control, a pneumatic-powered forming platen, manual sheet loading, fixed
clamp frames for sheet holding, a single-stage vacuum system and centrifugal fans. This
base setup provides reliable forming capability for many applications.
2. Technical Specifications & Price

configuration. All the machines are - closed chamber type meaning there is an air tight chamber
below the sheet line where a pulsated air maintains the sheet level during heating “sag control”
also known as “zero sag” - type machine.

Standard Fixed Clamp Frames: PF1 machines come standard with fixed-size clamping
frames. These frames hold the plastic sheet by its edges during forming. Changing sheet size
(format) requires manually repositioning and fastening the frame elements to match the new
sheet’s dimensions. Approximate time to adjust frames: 10–20 minutes, depending on the
size change and operator experience, since you must loosen, slide, and re-tighten multiple
segments on each side. Fixed frames are robust and simple, but the manual adjustment means
added downtime when switching between sheet sizes or part configurations.

1. Machine Overview
Machinecraft’s PF1 Series is a heavy-gauge, single-station cut-sheet thermoforming machine
line designed for versatility and high performance. All models feature a robust
closed-chamber design that prevents sheet sag by allowing pre-blow air pressure control,
ensuring superior forming quality on thick materials. The PF1 Series delivers precision forming
for a variety of thermoplastic sheets (typically 2–12 mm thick), making it ideal for automotive,
aerospace, industrial, and commercial applications that demand excellent detail and
consistency.
● Rugged Construction & Intelligent Control: Built with a heavy-duty steel frame and
precision motion components, the PF1 ensures stability and durability. A modern PLC
control system with an intuitive HMI touchscreen comes standard, allowing easy
control of heating zones, vacuum timing, and motion sequences.
● Classic Configuration: In its classic configuration, the PF1 includes sandwich heating
ovens in top & bottom from the sheet line - (ceramic infrared elements) with solid-state
temperature control, a pneumatic-powered forming platen, manual sheet loading, fixed
clamp frames for sheet holding, a single-stage vacuum system and centrifugal fans. This
base setup provides reliable forming capability for many applications.
2. Technical Specifications & Price
Below are the technical specifications for the PF1 Series selected options machine
configuration. All the machines are - closed chamber type meaning there is an air tight chamber
below the sheet line where a pulsated air maintains the sheet level during heating “sag control”
also known as “zero sag” - type machine.

Automatic Sheet Loader – Servo-Controlled Pick & Place System for Sheet Handling
The Machinecraft Automatic Sheet Loader is designed to automate the loading of large
thermoplastic sheets into the PF1-X vacuum forming machine. This optional module
improves cycle time, reduces labor dependency, and ensures consistent handling of
delicate or heavy plastic sheets.
How It Works:
contact@machinecraft.org 4

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
1. Pallet-Based Sheet Stack Input
○ Sheets are pre-loaded by the operator onto a pallet or tray in a designated
staging zone.
○ The autoloader is capable of handling multiple sheets in a single batch cycle.
2. Sheet Separation System
○ A pneumatic air-blow separator and a mechanical dancing frame
(tilt-style lifting mechanism) ensure only one sheet is picked up at a time.
3. Vacuum Pick-up & Transfer
○ Vacuum suction cups mounted on a servo-driven arm pick up the topmost
sheet.
○ The sheet is carried over and gently placed onto the forming area inside the
machine using a pick & place gantry with vertical and horizontal servo
motion.
4. Redundancy Check with Dual Sensors
○ Sensor 1 checks if a sheet has been picked.
○ Sensor 2 verifies if more than one sheet has been accidentally picked
(double-pick protection).
5. Simultaneous Part Unloading
○ While the next sheet is being loaded, the previously formed part is ejected
from the forming area using a top-frame-mounted pneumatic pusher
○ The part is moved to a tilted collection table for operator retrieval.
3.2 Heating System: There are 2 x heating ovens in the machine- independently
moving – Upper & Lower for Sandwich heating the thermoplastic sheet. The heater
elements are placed in a grid to cover the max. forming area

AM-P-5060 - Roll-Fed Pressure Forming Machine
Machine Overview
Continuous Roll-Fed Pressure Forming Machine
Forming Area: 500 × 600 mm
Application: Thick gauge packaging, automotive parts, deep draw containers

[PAGE 2]
The AM-P-5060 is designed for high-precision pressure forming of thick gauge plastics.
Features automatic sheet advancement, dual heating, pressure forming, and cutting in a single
continuous process with enhanced forming capability through vacuum and compressed air.
Key Features
• Roll-Fed System - Continuous operation with servo-driven spike chain • Pressure Forming -
Vacuum + compressed air for deep draws and fine details
• Sandwich Heating - Top and bottom infrared heaters for uniform heating • Eye Mark
Detection - Automatic alignment for pre-printed materials • Precision Control - Servo indexing
with ±0.3mm accuracy
Technical Specifications
Specification Details
Forming Area 500 × 600 mm
Max Forming Depth Up to 60 mm
Sheet Width 670 mm maximum
Sheet Thickness 0.5 - 2.0 mm
Materials HIPS, ABS, PVC, PET, PC
Production Speed Up to 10 cycles/min
Power Requirements 100 kW, 415V 3-Phase
Air Pressure 6 bar, clean & dry
Standard Configuration
• Roll Feeding System with servo-driven spike chain indexing
• Sandwich Heating System with top and bottom infrared heaters - multi pitch
• PID Temperature Control with multi-zone independent control
• Pressure Forming Station with vacuum and compressed air capability
• Automatic Cutting System with pneumatic shot cutter at exit
• PLC Control System with HMI touchscreen interface
• Safety Systems compliant with international standards

[PAGE 3]
Lead Time: 3 months from PO & advance payment
Payment Terms: 50% advance, 50% before dispatch
AM-P-5060 - Detailed Specifications & Pricing
Complete System Components
1. Roll Feeding System

Hi Nick,
Thank you for your detailed inquiry regarding the large thermoforming ovens. We
appreciate the opportunity to provide you with a quote. To ensure we offer the most
accurate and suitable solution for your needs, I have a few questions regarding your
specifications:
1. Sheet Loading & Unloading:
Could you clarify your preference for the sheet loading and unloading process? We offer
two primary options:
•Automated (Robotic): This system involves a robot placed at the side of the machine for
both loading and unloading. It is highly efficient for high-volume, continuous production
runs.
•Manual: For single or low-volume production, manual loading and unloading is often
preferred by our clients due to the setup time required for the automated system.
2. Minimum Sheet Size & Universal Frames:
To help us recommend the best clamping frame configuration, could you please specify
the minimum sheet size you intend to process?
The standard reduction in our universal frames is 1000 mm in the length direction and
500 mm in the depth direction. This design maintains a practical distance of
approximately one meter from the front of the machine, allowing for convenient operator
access. Further reductions can make it challenging for the operator to load and unload
the sheet.
For significant size variations, we typically recommend a multiple-frame solution. For
example, with a maximum size of 3200 x 2000 mm, a practical approach would be:
•Universal Frame 1: Accommodates sizes from 3200 x 2000 mm down to 2200 x 1500
mm.
•Universal Frame 2: Accommodates sizes from 2200 x 1500 mm down to 1200 x 1000
mm.
This ensures both flexibility and operator convenience.

3. Machine Movements:

though the movements are slightly slower compared to servo motors.

standard maximum depths are 500 mm, 650 mm, and 800 mm. Please let us know which
of these options would best suit your requirements.

accurate quote.
We look forward to hearing from you.
